$meta.purpose: Machine-readable design rules for AI-driven design generation

formatNote: Canonical values are as-authored (oklch/rgb/hex). Do not convert when reproducing.

primary.usage: Use for primary action buttons, text links, active navigation items, and focus outline rings.

cloudflareOrange.usage: Use strictly for logos, branding accents, and promotional notices. Do not use for standard UI controls.

foreground.usage: Default text color for primary headings, body text, and input content.

card.usage: Background color for surface containers (cards, dropdown lists, dialog overlays).

popover.usage: Background color for dropdown lists and tooltips.

secondary.usage: Background color for secondary buttons, pills, and pill badges.

secondary-foreground.usage: Text color for secondary actions and items.

muted.usage: Background color for inactive controls, code blocks, or disabled buttons.

muted-foreground.usage: Text color for secondary headers, captions, metadata, input placeholders, and keyboard shortcut tips.

accent.usage: Background color for elements on hover (such as list items or ghost buttons).

accent-foreground.usage: Text color for elements on hover (usually primary blue in light mode, foreground in dark).

destructive.usage: Background or outline color for error panels, alerts, and destructive actions.

border.usage: Color for hairline borders, card divides, and input outlines.

ring.usage: Color of the outline ring on focus states.

primary.usage: Sidebar primary indicator or icon accent.

pairing.rule: Always pair primary background with primary-foreground to satisfy accessibility guidelines.
Card containers must default to default foreground text.
Secondary buttons or badges should pair secondary background with secondary-foreground.
